name: substack-publisher
notes:
SETUP GUIDE

1. HOW TO IMPORT
   n8n Settings → Workflows → Import → paste this JSON

2. INSTALL COMMUNITY NODE (REQUIRED)
   n8n → Settings → Community Nodes → Install → search 'n8n-nodes-substack' → Install
   Restart n8n after installation if prompted.
   Source: https://www.npmjs.com/package/n8n-nodes-substack

3. ADD SUBSTACK CREDENTIAL
   n8n → Credentials → Add → Substack → paste your session cookie value
   Name it: 'Substack Sameer'
   How to get the session cookie:
     a. Log into Substack in your browser
     b. Open DevTools → Application tab → Storage → Cookies → substack.com
     c. Find the cookie named 'substack.sid'
     d. Copy the value and paste it into the n8n credential

4. SESSION COOKIE ROTATION (MONTHLY)
   IMPORTANT: Substack session cookies expire. Set a monthly reminder to:
     a. Log into Substack in browser
     b. Re-export the 'substack.sid' cookie value
     c. Update the n8n Substack credential
   The error handler in this workflow sends a Slack alert when the cookie has expired.

5. WHAT THIS WORKFLOW PUBLISHES
   Substack Notes ONLY (short-form, conversational posts)
   Long-form newsletter posts require manual publish from the Substack editor.
   This is intentional for v1 — Notes are better for regular engagement anyway.

6. HOW TO ACTIVATE
   After community node install and credential setup: toggle to Active

7. HOW TO TEST
   - Manually update any content_bank row status to 'approved' with platform_angles.substack set
   - The workflow will publish a Substack Note with the substack intro text + booking CTA
   - Check the 'Update content_bank with note result' node output for confirmation

8. TROUBLESHOOTING
   - 'Node type n8n-nodes-substack.substack not found': Community node not installed
     → n8n Settings → Community Nodes → Install n8n-nodes-substack → restart n8n
   - 'Unauthorized' or 'Authentication failed': Session cookie expired
     → Re-export substack.sid from browser DevTools → update n8n credential
   - Slack alert 'Substack publishing failed': Check n8n execution log for the specific error

nodes.name: Trigger: Supabase content_bank approved
nodes.notes: Watches content_bank table for INSERT and UPDATE events. The IF filter below restricts to status=approved rows only. Triggers when the Slack approval workflow sets status=approved.

nodes.name: Filter: status = approved
nodes.notes: Only proceed when content_bank row status = 'approved'. Ignores pending_review, draft, rejected, and published states.

nodes.name: Filter: has Substack content
nodes.notes: Only proceed when platform_angles.substack is populated. The AI content generation pipeline (Phase 10-03) should populate this field with the opening paragraph / Notes post text.

nodes.name: Prepare Substack Note content
nodes.notes: Builds the Substack Note body: substack intro paragraph + booking CTA link. The platform_angles.substack field should contain a conversational opening paragraph (not a full newsletter — that's what Notes are for).

nodes.name: Publish Substack Note
nodes.notes: Publishes a Substack Note using the n8n-nodes-substack community node. Authentication uses session cookie stored in the 'Substack Sameer' credential. Notes are short-form conversational posts — NOT long-form newsletter posts. Long-form posts require manual publish from the Substack editor.

nodes.name: Fallback: Community Node Not Installed

assignments.name: error_message
assignments.value: Substack publishing failed. Either: (1) n8n-nodes-substack community node is not installed — install from n8n Settings → Community Nodes → search n8n-nodes-substack, OR (2) Substack session cookie has expired — re-export substack.sid from browser DevTools and update the n8n Substack credential.

nodes.notes: Error fallback when the Substack community node is missing or session cookie has expired. Routes to Slack error alert. The onError: continueErrorOutput on the Publish Substack Note node sends errors here instead of failing the workflow.

nodes.name: Slack Alert: Substack publishing failed
nodes.notes: Sends a Slack alert to #content-pipeline when Substack publishing fails. Most common cause: session cookie expiry (happens monthly). The alert message includes the topic title and a plain-English fix description. Channel ID C0AGG5L97EG = #content-pipeline.

nodes.name: Store Substack Note result
nodes.notes: Captures the Substack Note result. The community node may return different field names depending on version (id, note_id, url, canonical_url). Uses || fallbacks to handle variations. published_at timestamp for audit trail.

nodes.name: Update content_bank with note result

fieldValues.fieldValue: ={{ JSON.stringify({ ...($('Trigger: Supabase content_bank approved').item.json.platform_angles || {}), substack_note_id: $json.substack_note_id, substack_note_url: $json.substack_note_url, substack_published_at: $json.published_at }) }}

nodes.notes: Updates content_bank.platform_angles JSONB to include substack_note_id, substack_note_url, and substack_published_at. Merges with existing platform_angles to preserve all platform content. The community node response format may vary — null url is acceptable.

nodes.name: Log: No Substack content (skip)

assignments.name: skipped_reason
assignments.value: No platform_angles.substack content available — AI content generation pipeline (Phase 10-03) must populate this field before Substack publishing can occur.

nodes.notes: Graceful skip when no Substack content is available. The AI generation pipeline should populate platform_angles.substack with an opening paragraph before this workflow triggers.
